data.title: Bare Skin and Laser offers brain stimulation for depression, anxiety, and mental fog. Launch party on Aug 19.
data.description: Celebrate the arrival of a new treatment at Bare Skin and Laser in Washington, Pennsylvania on August 19th with a complimentary launch party. This event will showcase a cutting-edge solution for individuals coping with depression, anxiety, or mental fog, offering medication-free relief. Join us for the unveiling of this innovative treatment that aims to provide hope and healing to those in need. Don't miss this opportunity to explore a new path towards well-being.

data.title: FAIR supports terminating sanctuary funding, citing risks of violating anti-harboring laws. - 81 characters
data.description: Local policies have been found to contradict federal mandates and may be in danger of breaching federal anti-harboring laws, as per the Federation for American Immigration Reform (FAIR), the country's leading public interest group supporting immigration enforcement and rational policies. The report released on August 7, 2025, highlights concerns about the legality of these policies and the potential consequences of violating federal regulations. The FAIR emphasizes the importance of adhering to federal guidelines to avoid legal repercussions, citing the risk of non-compliance with anti-harboring laws.

data.title: Elanco Animal Health beats profit expectations with EPS of 26 cents (down 13%) & revenue of $1.24 billion (5% growth). EBITDA at $238 million in Q2 2025. Sales growth at 8% on organic basis. Pet Health revenue up at $643 million, Farm Animal revenue down at $583 million due to aqua business sale.
data.description: Elanco Animal Health Inc. (NYSE: ELAN) reported Q2 2025 results with adjusted earnings per share at 26 cents, down 13% from the previous year. However, this exceeded the company's guidance (17-21 cents) and consensus estimate (20 cents). Revenue was $1.24 billion, surpassing the consensus estimate of $1.19 billion and management's guidance of $1.175-$1.195 billion. Sales grew 5% on a reported basis and 8% organically. Adjusted EBITDA was $238 million, a 13.5% decrease. Pet Health revenue was $643 million, up 11% reported and 10% organically, with a 4% price increase. Farm Animal revenue was $583 million, down 2% due to the sale of the aqua business. New products drove volume increases, with the Advantage Family and Seresto contributing revenue of $147 million and $113 million, respectively.

data.title: LabConnect partners with Kits4Life to reduce kit waste, advance sustainable clinical trials, promoting environmental stewardship.
data.description: Johnson City, Tennessee - On August 7, 2025, LabConnect disclosed their collaboration with Kits4Life, an endeavor by the MedSurplus Alliance. The partnership reflects LabConnect's dedication to environmental sustainability and prominence in eco-conscious clinical research.

data.title: Security Bancorp, Inc. releases Q2 earnings report, holding company for Security Federal Savings Bank of McMinnville, Tennessee. OTCPK: SCYT.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, Security Bancorp, Inc. (OTCBB: "SCYT"), the parent company of Security Federal Savings Bank in McMinnville, Tennessee, revealed their financial results for the second quarter of the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2025.

data.title: Inventor from Stuart, Fla. creates Diaper Changing Station for Vehicles (FJK-350) to improve safety and cleanliness.
data.description: A new invention called the Diaper Changing Station for Vehicle has been created by an inventor from Stuart, Florida, who sought to improve the process of changing a baby's diaper in a vehicle. The innovator believed there was a need for a safer, cleaner, and more convenient solution. This design aims to enhance sanitation and eliminate the challenges often faced when changing diapers on-the-go.Introduced in Pittsburgh on Aug. 7, 2025, the product has garnered attention for its practicality. The innovative station provides a new way for parents to manage diaper changes while traveling.

data.title: Logistics Plus named exclusive logistics provider of the Joe Moore Award, transporting trophy worldwide. ERIE, Pa. Aug. 7, 2025.
data.description: Logistics Plus has been chosen as the official logistics sponsor to handle the transportation and delivery of the prestigious Joe Moore Award trophy. The company, based in ERIE, Pa., made the announcement on August 7, 2025. Logistics Plus, a renowned name in transportation and logistics, will handle all aspects of shipping the massive trophy. This partnership ensures the secure and timely delivery of the award.

data.title: 2026 Grand Wagoneer to be launched later this year, highlighting new updates and attitude, as Jeep celebrates 85 years as SUV leader.
data.description: A new version of the Jeep® Grand Wagoneer is set to be released later this year in 2026, as confirmed in statements from Auburn Hills, Mich., on August 7, 2025. Promising a refreshed look and a new attitude, this model proudly showcases the Jeep badging. Jeep, an established brand with nearly 85 years of experience, is known for its excellence in SUVs and is recognized globally for its top-notch off-road capabilities.

data.title: Apple commits additional $100 billion to US manufacturing to avoid tariffs, plans total $600 billion investment over 4 years.
data.description: Apple Inc. has announced an additional $100 billion commitment to U.S. manufacturing, bringing their total planned U.S. investment to $600 billion over four years. The move is seen as strategic to avoid tariffs under President Trump's trade policies and potentially boost the company's stocks, which are currently down 12% year-to-date. The American Manufacturing Program (AMP) aims to localize Apple's supply chain within the U.S. and includes partnerships with 10 domestic companies. The timing of this announcement coincided with Trump's unveiling of a tariff on semiconductors, from which Apple is now exempt due to their increased U.S. commitment. This not only serves as a patriotic investment but also a calculated step to protect the iPhone business from tariff impacts that could affect pricing, margins, and competitiveness. Analysis by Wall Street sees this move as a tactical maneuver by Apple.

data.title: Realty Income Corp (NYSE: O) reported Q2 FY2025 financial results with 639,000 sq ft leased, operating property lease rate at 79.1%, occupancy rate at 77.4% and weighted average lease term at 5.5 years. CEO highlighted progress in new business strategy and lease activity.
data.description: Realty Income Corp. released its Q2 2025 financial results, with chief executive officer Paul McDowell highlighting 639,000 square feet of leasing completed as of July 31st. This includes new and renewal transactions with a weighted average lease term of 6.4 years. Specifically, there were three new leases in the quarter: a 15.7-year agreement for 46,000 square feet in New Jersey, a 5.4-year agreement for 80,000 square feet in Georgia, and a 7.6-year agreement for 23,000 square feet in Texas. The operating property occupancy rate was 77.4% at quarter-end, up 310 basis points sequentially. Operating property lease rate was 79.1%, up 170 basis points sequentially. Weighted average lease term increased to 5.5 years from 5.2 years last quarter and 4.2 years a year ago. The company anticipates tenant retention fluctuations due to portfolio size and timings of move-outs and projects a rise in portfolio occupancy post-2025.

data.title: US Foods (NYSE: USFD) reports Q2 EPS of $1.19 (beat est. $1.13) with sales of $10.082B (up 3.8% YoY). Maintains revenue outlook.
data.description: US Foods Holding Corp. (NYSE: USFD) saw a decline in stock value following the release of their second-quarter financial results. The company offers a variety of products under brands such as Chef's Line, Stock Yards, Metro Deli, and others, along with distributing foodservice equipment under brands like Chef'Stores and Food Fanatics. Their second-quarter adjusted earnings per share stood at $1.19, surpassing the expected $1.13. Despite this positive result, quarterly sales of $10.082 billion, showing a 3.8% increase from last year, fell short of the estimated $10.166 billion. CFO Dirk Locascio expressed satisfaction, citing top-line growth, margin expansion, and a 28% rise in Adjusted EPS aided by beneficial share buybacks. More details can be found at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Vay partners with Bluedot for electric fleet charging. Focus on remote driving tech. Improving city mobility with practical solutions.
data.description: In Palo Alto, California on August 7, 2025, Vay, a top provider of automotive-grade remote driving technology, is working towards a vision where remote driving can significantly enhance urban mobility. This innovation aims to present a viable option to private car ownership and ride-hailing services. By utilizing this technology, cities could potentially experience a transformative shift in their transportation systems.
